@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent mistakenly identified 'food' domain papers by regex-matching the word "food" in full-text documents (call_XqM4EBEct83OU2WR077vMLxZ) and then summing citations for those titles (call_9yuHbjx0se9TGksu2Pv1OztN via call_yWLJz5B6dvNqKsIRlKHsC4gF), instead of extracting and filtering by the domain metadata, thereby including non-food-domain papers and inflating the total.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried paper_docs_database for any paper whose full text contained the word "food" (var_call_oHqRL9azfQPZYlORB1e7qn5h) and summed citations for those titles (using aggregated Citations data in var_call_bUQQJpWTgmYt2geHH5eT5q3o via var_call_0XxLnjB44rvbEMacTJKm26rL), selecting papers by the wrong field rather than extracting/using the actual domain attribute, thus overcounting and producing 14927 instead of 11203.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered papers by searching for the keyword "food" anywhere in the full text (query_db on paper_docs with a regex on the text field) instead of extracting and using the 'domain' metadata to identify 'food' domain papers, then summed citations for those titles, resulting in an overinclusive count.

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent inferred 'food' domain papers by filtering Mongo documents only for the keyword "food" and then applying a heuristic threshold on 'food'/'diet' mentions (execute_python after the Mongo query), rather than extracting and matching the domain label, causing inclusion/exclusion errors and an incorrect total citation sum.
